chief_complaint: An 85-year-old patient hospitalized for influenza-associated pneumonia developed a sudden febrile coma.
history: The patient was hospitalized for influenza-associated pneumonia. A sudden febrile coma developed, creating a complex diagnostic challenge. A bacterial co-infection was considered due to the patient's age and comorbidities.
exam: Vital signs: febrile coma. Physical examination findings: not specified.
labs: Cerebrospinal fluid analysis and multiplex PCR revealed Listeria monocytogenes.
